sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Cagliari are struggling, having failed to win in their last 4 matches, with 2 draws and 2 losses, though they showed resilience with a late 2-2 draw against Hellas Verona. Sassuolo's form has been mixed, with 2 wins, 1 draw, and 2 losses in their last 5, their latest result being a narrow 0-1 loss to Roma. Both teams have struggled defensively, with Cagliari without a clean sheet in 6 games and Sassuolo conceding in 3 of their last 5.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two encounters, Cagliari have dominated Sassuolo, winning both matches (2-0 away and 2-1 at home). Cagliari have won 3 of the last 5 head-to-heads, with historical trends showing low-scoring games, as 6 of the last 8 matches had under 2.5 goals.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Cagliari and Sassuolo are closely matched in the standings, with Cagliari at 14th (9 points) and Sassuolo at 13th (10 points), both having scored 8 goals this season. Cagliari's strength lies in late goals (50% in the 76-90th minute range) and a high big chance conversion rate (50%), while Sassuolo struggle with creativity, ranking low in final third entries and penalty area touches.

sections.subtitle: Match Outlook
sections.text: Given Cagliari's home advantage, recent head-to-head success, and Sofascore's 46% probability of a home win, they are slight favorites. However, Sassuolo's ability to score late (22% of goals in 76-90th minute) could pose a threat if Cagliari fail to close out the game. Expect a tight, low-scoring match with a high likelihood of cards due to both teams’ foul-heavy playstyles.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: The first half between Cagliari and Sassuolo at the Unipol Domus ended in a goalless draw, with neither team managing to break the deadlock. Both sides showed moments of promise but struggled to convert their opportunities into goals. Sassuolo dominated possession and created more entries into the final third, while Cagliari displayed resilience in defense and showed efficiency in duels. The game remains finely poised, with both teams likely eager to capitalize on any lapses in concentration in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance: Cagliari
halftimeSections.text: Cagliari had less possession in the first half but made the most of their defensive structure, winning a significant portion of duels. Their entries into the final third were decent, though they failed to register any shots on target. Players like Riyad Idrissi and Michael Folorunsho showed intent with key passes, but the lack of clinical finishing was evident. Defensively, they held firm with notable contributions in clearances and tackles, keeping Sassuolo's attacking threats at bay for the most part.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance: Sassuolo
halftimeSections.text: Sassuolo controlled the ball for the majority of the first half and looked more threatening in the final third, managing a couple of shots on target. Their penalty area entries suggest a more aggressive approach, though they couldn’t convert these into goals. Kristian Thorstvedt stood out with key passes, while Cristian Volpato showed danger with his attempts. However, their duel-winning statistics were less impressive, indicating a potential vulnerability if Cagliari ramps up the physicality in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw fluctuating attacking momentum between both teams. Sassuolo started with early intent, pushing forward in the opening minutes before Cagliari responded with a strong spell of pressure around the middle of the half. Sassuolo regained the upper hand with a sustained period of dominance as the half progressed, particularly in the latter stages. Cagliari had their moments, especially with brief attacking bursts, but couldn’t maintain consistency. The momentum shifts suggest Sassuolo might carry more confidence into the second half, though Cagliari’s sporadic surges could still pose a threat.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key areas to watch
halftimeSections.text: Discipline will be crucial in the second half, as both teams have already picked up a yellow card each, which could influence their approach to challenges. Cagliari needs to improve their attacking output and capitalize on their final third entries to test Sassuolo’s defense. Meanwhile, Sassuolo must maintain their possession advantage and be more clinical in the penalty area to break through Cagliari’s resolute backline. Set-pieces could also play a role, with Sassuolo earning more corners in the first half, which might be an avenue to exploit.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: Given the balance of play in the first half, the second half is likely to see a cautious start as both teams assess their opportunities. Sassuolo appears to have the slight edge due to their control of the game and ability to create chances, which could see them push for a breakthrough. Cagliari, playing at home, will likely rely on counter-attacks and look to exploit any defensive errors from Sassuolo. A single goal could open up the game, but both teams will need to sharpen their finishing to secure the lead.
